---
title: "Academic Activities"
---

```{=html}
<style>
/* Academic lists spacing & elegance */
ul {
  padding-left: 1.2rem;
}
li {
  margin-bottom: 0.8rem;
  line-height: 1.5;
}
.role-badge {
  font-family: var(--font-family-monospace);
  font-size: 0.75rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: #1b4332;
  background: #eaf5eb;
  padding: 0.15rem 0.4rem;
  border-radius: 4px;
  margin-left: 0.3rem;
  display: inline-block;
}
.role-badge-invited {
  font-family: var(--font-family-monospace);
  font-size: 0.75rem;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: #7c3aed;
  background: #f3e8ff;
  padding: 0.15rem 0.4rem;
  border-radius: 4px;
  margin-left: 0.3rem;
  display: inline-block;
}
</style>
```

*Full panel, paper, or roundtable details are available upon request.*


### Peer-Reviewed International Conferences and Workshops

* **American Political Science Association (APSA) Annual Meeting**, 2026 — Kamalov, E., **Nugumanova, K.**, & Sergeeva, I. (alphabetical order), *Transnational Repression and Engagement: Evidence from a Panel of Russian Exiles*. <span class="role-badge">Co-presenter, Chair & Discussant</span>
* **European Consortium for Political Research (ECPR) General Conference**, 2026 — Kamalov, E., **Nugumanova, K.**, & Sergeeva, I. (alphabetical order), *Transnational Repression and Engagement: Evidence from a Panel of Russian Exiles*. <span class="role-badge">Paper Presenter & Discussant</span>
* **European Consortium for Political Research (ECPR) General Conference**, 2025 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *Gendered Mobilization among Russian Emigrants in Exile*. <span class="role-badge">Paper Presenter</span>
* **Association for Slavic, East European, and Eurasian Studies (ASEEES) Annual Convention**, 2025 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *Micromobilizing Emotion: How Feminist Anti-War Resistance Builds Affective Infrastructure in Exile*. <span class="role-badge">Invited Roundtable Speaker & Discussant</span>
* **Association for Slavic, East European, and Eurasian Studies (ASEEES) Annual Convention**, 2024 — **Nugumanova, K.**, E. Kamalov, I. Sergeeva, *Gendered Response to War*. <span class="role-badge">Paper Presenter</span>
* **ISA / ESA / AIS Gens Conference**, 2025 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *Gender, War, and Political Action among Russian Exiles*. <span class="role-badge">Paper Presenter</span>
* **Gender, Peace and Security Workshop**, 2025 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *Gender, Peace, and Security among Russian Anti-War Emigrants*. <span class="role-badge">Paper Presenter (Acceptance rate ~3%)</span>
* **Aleksanteri Conference**, 2024 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *Feminism in Action: Political Power of the Feminist Antiwar Resistance*. <span class="role-badge">Paper Presenter</span>
* **Aleksanteri Conference**, 2023 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *Gendered Response to War*. <span class="role-badge">Paper Presenter</span>
* **Aleksanteri Conference**, 2023 — Kostenko, V., **Nugumanova, K.**, *Gender Differences between Post-War Emigrants from Russia: Guilt, Sorrow, Responsibility, and Hope*. <span class="role-badge">Сo-author of the presentation</span>
* **15th European Graduate Network Conference**, Zurich, 2024 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *Gendered Responses to War: Analyzing Differences and Underlying Factors in the Case of Russian Emigration, 2022–2023*. <span class="role-badge">Paper Presenter & Discussant</span>
* **Migration Working Group**, European University Institute, 2024 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *Gendered Responses to War: Analyzing Differences and Underlying Factors in the Case of Russian Emigration, 2022–2023*. <span class="role-badge">Paper Presenter</span>
* **LCSR International Workshop on Comparative Values**, 2024 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *Gendered Responses to War: Analyzing Differences and Underlying Factors in the Case of Russian Emigration, 2022–2023*. <span class="role-badge">Paper Presenter</span>
* **Problematizing Migration**, University of Palermo, 2023 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *Gender Differences in Attitudes, Coping Strategies, and Integration among Russian Emigrants*. <span class="role-badge">Paper Presenter</span>


### Invited International Talks and Expert Seminars

* **Russia Expert Seminar**, Robert Schuman Centre, EUI, 2025 — E. Kamalov, **Nugumanova, K.**, I. Sergeeva, *Russian Diaspora: Trends, Risks and Entrepreneurship*. <span class="role-badge-invited">Invited Seminar Speaker</span>
* **Migration and Diversity Workshop**, ZoiS, 2025 — **Nugumanova, K.**, E. Kamalov, I. Sergeeva, *Reverse Gender Gap in Transnational Activism: War-Driven Political Engagement among Russian Post-2022 Emigrants*. <span class="role-badge-invited">Invited Workshop Speaker</span>
* **Kovcheg Business.Network International Conference**, 2025 — E. Kamalov, **Nugumanova, K.**, I. Sergeeva, *To Stay or to Return: Mobility, Integration, and the Dynamics of Russian Emigration*. <span class="role-badge-invited">Featured Speaker</span>
* **Academic Bridges International Conference**, 2025 — E. Kamalov, **Nugumanova, K.**, I. Sergeeva, *Three Years in Emigration: Results of a Panel Study of the Lives of Russian Emigrants*. <span class="role-badge-invited">Invited Speaker</span>
* **Cross-Border Voices Workshop**, ZoiS & Ilia State University, 2024 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *Cross-Border Voices: Emigration, Gender, and Activism in the Wake of the Russian Invasion of Ukraine*. <span class="role-badge-invited">Invited Speaker</span>
* **Russian Journalists in Exile Workshop**, University of Helsinki & partners, 2024 — E. Kamalov, I. Sergeeva, **Nugumanova, K.**, *Can Migrant Organizations Evade the Long Arm of Autocrats? A Conjoint Experiment on Donation Preferences among Russian Exiles*. <span class="role-badge-invited">Invited Workshop Speaker</span>
* **New Russian Exodus and Host Societies: Impacts and Responses**, Yerevan State University, 2024 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *Armenia in Focus: A Safer Haven for Russians*. <span class="role-badge-invited">Invited Speaker</span>
* **In the Footsteps of Emigrants: In Search of a New World**, Academic Bridges, Kovcheg, Science at Risk, 2023 — **Nugumanova, K.**, *War Doesn't Have a Woman's Face? Political Behaviour and Civic Engagement of Russian Post-War Migrants*. <span class="role-badge-invited">Invited Speaker</span>


### Summer and Spring Schools

* **Spring School on Democratic Backsliding and Political Conflict.** European University Institute & Scuola Normale Superiore, Florence, 2026.
* **Experimental Designs in the Social and Behavioral Sciences: Multifactorial Survey Experiments.** *GESIS Spring Seminar 2025*, Cologne, Germany, March 31 – April 4, 2025.
* **SPS Summer Academy 2022: (In)equality, Solidarity and Democracy in Europe.** European University Institute (EUI), Florence, Italy, 2022.
* **Friedrich Naumann Foundation Online Summer School "Eclipse"** (Focus on anti-war mobilization and academic resistance), 2022.
* **Data Science Summer School.** Hertie School Data Science Lab, Berlin (online), 2022.


### Professional Trainings

* **JSPS e-Learning Course on Research Ethics.** Japan Society for the Promotion of Science (JSPS), 2026.
* **5-Day Training for Interviewers** under the auspices of the International Project *"Building the Anti-war Commons in the Russian Diaspora"*, Helsinki, Finland, 2022.
* **88-Hour Professional Career Track: Data Scientist with R.** DataCamp, 2022.
* **Research, Ethics, Compliance, and Safety Training — CITI Program:** *Human Research: Social/Behavioral Researchers* (2022); *COVID-19: Mental Health for Higher Ed and Healthcare* (2022).
* **EY Business School: Introduction to Auditing.** Ernst & Young (EY), Russia, 2019.


### Online Courses and Certifications

* **Migration and Health: Enhancing Intercultural Competence and Diversity Sensitivity.** OpenWHO / World Health Organization (WHO), 2023.
* **Human Rights, Human Wrongs: Challenging Poverty, Vulnerability and Social Exclusion.** SDGAcademyX, 2023.
* **Gender, Violence and Post-Conflict States.** University of Iceland, 2022.
* **Qualitative Research Methods.** University of Amsterdam, 2022.
* **Feminism and Social Justice.** University of California, Santa Cruz, 2020.
* **Introduction to Tableau.** DataCamp, 2022.
